_disclaimer: APPROXIMATE DATA — AI campus locations and details are public estimates from press releases, news, and company announcements. Not audited or officially verified. Replace with official public datasets before policy use.
_sources: Company press releases and investor relations (public)

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft AI - Loudoun County AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Dedicated AI training cluster adjacent to existing cloud campus.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft AI - Racine AI Supercomputing Campus
ai_campuses.notes: $3.3B AI-specific campus; 100MW+ GPU clusters announced 2023.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft AI - Hendricks County AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Part of Indiana AI infrastructure push; dedicated training.

ai_campuses.name: Meta AI - Altoona AI Research Campus
ai_campuses.notes: FAIR (Fundamental AI Research) compute cluster; Grand Teton AI supercomputer.

ai_campuses.name: Meta AI - New Albany AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: AI/Llama model training; next-gen GPU clusters.

ai_campuses.name: Meta AI - Fort Worth AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Dedicated AI inference and training infrastructure.

ai_campuses.name: Google AI - The Dalles TPU Cluster
ai_campuses.notes: Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) v4 pods; Gemini training infrastructure.

ai_campuses.name: Google AI - Council Bluffs AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: TPU training pods; Bard/Gemini model infrastructure.

ai_campuses.name: Google AI - Berkeley County AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: AI inference cluster; South Carolina 100% renewable.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon AI - Ashburn Trainium Cluster
ai_campuses.notes: AWS Trainium/Inferentia chip cluster; Bedrock model hosting.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon AI - Umatilla AI Cluster
ai_campuses.notes: Trainium2 training cluster; us-west-2 AI zone.

ai_campuses.name: xAI - Memphis Supercomputer (Colossus)
ai_campuses.notes: 100,000+ H100 GPUs; largest AI training cluster publicly announced as of 2024; Shelby County Memphis.

ai_campuses.name: NVIDIA AI Research - Austin Campus
ai_campuses.notes: AI research and development; GPU architecture lab.

ai_campuses.name: CoreWeave AI - Parsippany Campus
ai_campuses.notes: GPU-as-a-service; massive NVIDIA H100 cluster for AI startups.

ai_campuses.name: CoreWeave AI - Chicago Campus
ai_campuses.notes: DuPage County; NVIDIA AI cluster; cloud AI infrastructure.

ai_campuses.name: Anthropic AI - San Francisco Area (cloud)
ai_campuses.notes: AI safety research; training conducted via cloud partnerships.

ai_campuses.name: Oracle AI Cloud - Austin Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Oracle AI cloud infrastructure; $1B+ investment in Texas.

ai_campuses.name: Oracle AI Cloud - Nashville Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Nashville; AI/ML workloads.

ai_campuses.name: Cerebras AI - Santa Clara HQ
ai_campuses.notes: Wafer-scale engine AI training; custom CS-3 chips.

ai_campuses.name: Argonne National Lab - AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Aurora exascale supercomputer (AI workloads); 2 exaflops.

ai_campuses.name: Oak Ridge National Lab - AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Frontier exascale supercomputer; #1 Top500 2022-2023.

ai_campuses.name: NERSC - Lawrence Berkeley AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Perlmutter AI-optimized supercomputer; NVIDIA A100 GPUs.

ai_campuses.name: Stargate AI Campus — Abilene (Phase 1)
ai_campuses.notes: First Stargate campus; announced January 2025. $500B AI infrastructure joint venture. Hundreds of acres adjacent to ERCOT transmission. Purpose-built for frontier AI model training. AEP Texas provides power.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft AI — Quincy Data Center Expansion
ai_campuses.notes: Microsoft's largest data center campus; multiple generations of expansion since 2006. Dedicated AI training clusters (Azure AI). BPA hydroelectric power. Grant County PUD provides lowest-cost power in the state.

ai_campuses.name: Switch SUPERNAP — Las Vegas AI Cluster
ai_campuses.notes: Switch SUPERNAP 7-10 campus; dedicated GPU cluster for AI/ML workloads. 100MW+ capacity. NVIDIA DGX-A100/H100 pods. Tier IV design.

ai_campuses.name: Meta AI — Eagle Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: 1.9 million sq ft campus in Eagle, ID (Ada County). Grand Teton AI computing infrastructure for FAIR research and Llama model training. 100% renewable power from Idaho Power hydroelectric and wind.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft AI — Oklahoma City Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Part of Microsoft's announced $3B+ investment in Oklahoma AI infrastructure. Dedicated Azure AI / Copilot training clusters. OG&E power. Multiple campus phases announced 2024-2025.

ai_campuses.name: Google AI — Douglas County Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Google's primary Atlanta-area AI training campus in Douglasville. TPU v4/v5 pods for Gemini model training. 100% renewable energy. Major capacity expansions ongoing.

ai_campuses.name: Google DeepMind / Google AI — Council Bluffs Expansion
ai_campuses.notes: Expansion of existing Council Bluffs campus with dedicated TPU pods for Gemini Ultra and Gemini Flash training. $600M+ expansion announced 2024.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft AI — North Dakota Hyperscale Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Announced 2024 as part of Microsoft's Midwest AI expansion. Cass County (Fargo area). Power provided by Xcel Energy. Dedicated AI inference cluster.

ai_campuses.name: Switch SUPERNAP Michigan — Grand Rapids AI Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Switch Michigan campus (Grand Rapids). 1 million+ sq ft. AI/GPU cluster for cloud AI workloads. Consumers Energy power. Michigan PA 328 incentive.

ai_campuses.name: Crusoe Energy AI Cloud — Denver Metro Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Crusoe's primary Front Range AI cloud campus. NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD clusters. Serves AI startups and frontier model training. Xcel Energy power.

ai_campuses.name: Google Pryor Creek Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: One of Google's largest campuses globally; MidAmerica Industrial Park, Pryor OK. Multi-billion-dollar investment.

ai_campuses.name: Meta Gallatin Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Meta data center campus in Gallatin, Sumner County TN. ~$800M investment. Renewable energy powered.

ai_campuses.name: Meta DeKalb Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Meta data center in DeKalb, IL. ~$800M investment. Largest employer in DeKalb County.

ai_campuses.name: Meta Los Lunas Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Meta data center campus in Los Lunas, Valencia County NM. $1B+ investment; major western US facility.

ai_campuses.name: Meta Huntsville Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Meta data center campus near Huntsville, Madison County AL. One of Meta's largest southeastern US facilities.

ai_campuses.name: Apple Prineville Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Apple's first and flagship owned data center. Prineville, Crook County OR. ~$1B investment. 100% renewable energy.

ai_campuses.name: Apple Waukee Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Apple data center in Waukee, Dallas County IA. $1.3B+ campus; Apple's second Iowa facility.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft San Antonio Lone Star Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Microsoft's Lone Star Campus in San Antonio, Bexar County TX. One of Microsoft's largest global campuses. Ongoing multi-billion-dollar expansion.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon AWS Clarksville Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Amazon Web Services data center campus in Clarksville, Montgomery County TN. Part of AWS's expanding southeastern US region.

ai_campuses.name: Meta Stanton Springs Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Meta data center in Stanton Springs Technology Park, Jackson County GA. Part of the four-county Stanton Springs industrial park initiative.

ai_campuses.name: Iron Mountain / QTS Richmond Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Hyperscale campus in Henrico County, VA. One of QTS's flagship properties, acquired by Blackstone/Iron Mountain in 2021. Multiple hyperscale buildings.

ai_campuses.name: CyrusOne Minneapolis-St. Paul Metro Campus
ai_campuses.notes: CyrusOne data center campus in Eagan, Dakota County, MN. Core of the Twin Cities enterprise data center market.

ai_campuses.name: Google Utah Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Google announced a $1B+ data center campus in the Salt Lake City metro area in 2024. Part of Google's continued US hyperscale expansion.

ai_campuses.name: Iron Mountain Wilmington Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Iron Mountain (formerly IO Data Centers) colocation facility in New Castle County, Delaware. Serves the Mid-Atlantic enterprise market.

ai_campuses.name: T-Mobile Sioux Falls Network Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: T-Mobile major network operations data center in Sioux Falls, Minnehaha County SD. Anchors the Sioux Falls technology/data center ecosystem.

ai_campuses.name: Windstream / Lumos Winston-Salem Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Windstream and Lumos Networks carrier-grade colocation facilities in Winston-Salem, Forsyth County NC. Anchor of the Winston-Salem data center corridor.

ai_campuses.name: CyrusOne Pompano Beach Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Major enterprise and wholesale colocation campus in Broward County FL.

ai_campuses.name: Google Channahon AI Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Google's largest Midwest data center campus; expanded multiple times since 2016.

ai_campuses.name: AT&T Overland Park Network Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: AT&T Central Office and data center serving the Kansas City metro.

ai_campuses.name: AWS Cincinnati Region Infrastructure
ai_campuses.notes: Amazon Web Services regional data center supporting Ohio cloud infrastructure.

ai_campuses.name: Novva Data Centers — North Salt Lake Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Purpose-built 120+ MW hyperscale campus in Davis County Utah.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon AWS Chesterfield Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: AWS data center cluster in Chesterfield County south of Richmond VA.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft Marysville AI Infrastructure Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Microsoft expanding Puget Sound AI training campus in Snohomish County.

ai_campuses.name: Equinix SV1 — Redwood City Silicon Valley Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Equinix's flagship Silicon Valley data center, former PAIX internet exchange hub.

ai_campuses.name: Flexential Colorado Springs Defense Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Enterprise and government colocation near Peterson/Schriever Space Force Bases.

ai_campuses.name: Expedient Orlando Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Central Florida enterprise colocation facility serving hospitality and healthcare IT.

ai_campuses.name: Annapolis Junction Defense IT Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Unclassified contractor data center cluster adjacent to NSA/Fort Meade in Annapolis Junction MD.

ai_campuses.name: Expedient Cleveland Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Cuyahoga County healthcare IT colocation hub serving Cleveland Clinic and University Hospitals.

ai_campuses.name: CyrusOne Sugar Land Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Houston-area overflow data center campus in Fort Bend County serving energy sector clients.

ai_campuses.name: Meta Newnan Data Center Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Meta hyperscale data center in Coweta County GA, I-85 southwest Atlanta corridor.

ai_campuses.name: Equinix NY7 Newark Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Major internet exchange and colocation facility; among top 5 IX points in North America.

ai_campuses.name: Equinix BO1 Boston Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Flagship New England data center; financial services and biotech AI workloads.

ai_campuses.name: Expedient Pittsburgh Data Center — Strip District
ai_campuses.notes: Pittsburgh flagship campus; CMU AI research proximity drives HPC demand.

ai_campuses.name: TierPoint Fort Mill Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Charlotte metro overflow enterprise colocation in Fort Mill, York County SC.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft Grand Forks Hyperscale Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Multi-billion-dollar hyperscale campus; one of the largest private investments in North Dakota.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon HQ2 National Landing — AWS Infrastructure Hub
ai_campuses.notes: Amazon HQ2 in Crystal City/National Landing Arlington VA with adjacent AWS operations.

ai_campuses.name: QTS Spokane Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Eastern Washington data center leveraging Avista hydroelectric power.

ai_campuses.name: Mayo Clinic AI and Clinical Informatics Platform
ai_campuses.notes: One of the largest healthcare AI workloads in the US; partnered with Google Cloud and AWS.

ai_campuses.name: Florida State Data Center — Tallahassee
ai_campuses.notes: Consolidated state agency IT infrastructure hub under Florida's §282.201 mandate.

ai_campuses.name: Windstream / Kinetic Business Network Data Center — Little Rock
ai_campuses.notes: Windstream HQ campus; major mid-South fiber/telecom network data center.

ai_campuses.name: Cologix New Orleans Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Primary carrier-neutral internet exchange and Gulf energy sector colocation hub.

ai_campuses.name: Equinix STL1 St. Louis Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Primary Midwest internet exchange west of Chicago; Mastercard and Centene enterprise hub.

ai_campuses.name: Tier1 Cloud Greensboro Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Enterprise colocation serving VF Corp, HanesBrands and Piedmont NC corridor.

ai_campuses.name: Williams Companies Tulsa Operations Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Critical pipeline SCADA and midstream operations IT infrastructure in Tulsa.

ai_campuses.name: GCI Data Center — Anchorage
ai_campuses.notes: Primary commercial data center in Alaska; serves enterprise and government clients across the state.

ai_campuses.name: DRFortress Honolulu Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Hawaii's largest commercial data center; carrier-neutral, Pacific subsea cable interconnection hub.

ai_campuses.name: Lumen Technologies Jackson Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Colocation and network hub serving Mississippi state government and enterprise clients.

ai_campuses.name: Walmart Global Technology Data Center — Bentonville
ai_campuses.notes: On-premises enterprise data center supporting Walmart's global retail technology and supply-chain AI operations.

ai_campuses.name: DataBank Eastern Panhandle Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Edge data center serving DC-metro overspill demand in Martinsburg WV; benefits from WV EDGE Act incentives.

ai_campuses.name: UAF Arctic Region Supercomputing Center (ARSC)
ai_campuses.notes: High-performance computing center supporting Arctic research, climate modeling, and DoD/NOAA computation workloads.

ai_campuses.name: Maui High Performance Computing Center (MHPCC)
ai_campuses.notes: DoD-funded HPC center operated for the Air Force Research Laboratory; supports Pacific theater defense simulation and AI workloads.

ai_campuses.name: Delaware DTI State Data Center — Dover
ai_campuses.operator: Delaware Department of Technology and Information
ai_campuses.notes: Primary state government data center serving Delaware's agency IT infrastructure, hosting enterprise applications and disaster recovery.

ai_campuses.name: Alabama Supercomputer Authority Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: State-operated HPC facility providing supercomputing resources for Alabama universities, state agencies, and research programs.

ai_campuses.name: Immedion Charleston Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Regional colocation data center serving Charleston's growing financial technology, healthcare, and logistics IT sectors.

ai_campuses.name: Qualcomm Data Center — San Diego
ai_campuses.notes: On-premises data center supporting Qualcomm's semiconductor R&D, 5G/AI chip testing, and enterprise compute operations.

ai_campuses.name: Nissan North America IT Operations Center
ai_campuses.notes: Nissan's North American IT hub in Smyrna/Rutherford County supporting manufacturing systems, supply chain AI, and connected vehicle data.

ai_campuses.name: Adobe Systems Utah Data Center — Silicon Slopes
ai_campuses.notes: Adobe's Utah data center supporting Creative Cloud, Document Cloud, and Experience Cloud AI/ML workloads in the Silicon Slopes corridor.

ai_campuses.name: NDSU High Performance Computing Center
ai_campuses.notes: NDSU's HPC facility supporting agricultural data analytics, materials science research, and Great Plains climate modeling.

ai_campuses.name: M&T Bank Technology Operations Center
ai_campuses.notes: M&T Bank's primary technology operations hub supporting regional banking IT, data analytics, and financial services processing for the Buffalo-Niagara region.

ai_campuses.name: Lumen Technologies Aurora Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: Legacy CenturyLink/Lumen colocation and network hub serving the Denver metro; one of the largest carrier-neutral facilities in Arapahoe County.

ai_campuses.name: Comcast Technology Center — Philadelphia
ai_campuses.notes: Comcast's global technology headquarters in Center City Philadelphia; houses major data infrastructure for NBCUniversal, Xfinity, and enterprise services.

ai_campuses.name: Iron Mountain Data Center — Chicago West (Aurora)
ai_campuses.notes: Iron Mountain colocation facility in Aurora (Kane County) serving Chicago metro enterprise clients; part of the west suburban Chicago data center corridor.

ai_campuses.name: UGA Georgia Advanced Research Computing Center
ai_campuses.notes: UGA's GACRC provides HPC resources for genomics, climate research, and computational biology; houses Georgia Advanced Research Computing infrastructure.

ai_campuses.name: University of Notre Dame Center for Research Computing
ai_campuses.notes: Notre Dame CRC operates the Duda supercomputing cluster and AI/ML platforms supporting computational science, engineering, and social science research.

ai_campuses.name: New York State ITS Enterprise Data Center — Albany
ai_campuses.operator: NYS Office of Information Technology Services
ai_campuses.notes: State-operated enterprise data center providing IT infrastructure for New York State agencies; one of the largest public-sector data centers in the Northeast.

ai_campuses.name: GlobalFoundries Fab 8 Computing Infrastructure — Malta
ai_campuses.notes: On-premises HPC and EDA compute supporting chip design simulation, manufacturing AI, and yield analytics for one of North America's largest semiconductor fabs.

ai_campuses.name: Air Force Research Laboratory Computing Center — Wright-Patterson
ai_campuses.notes: AFRL operates DoD HPC resources at Wright-Patterson supporting materials science, aerospace modeling, autonomy AI, and cyber research across the Information, Space, and Sensors directorates.

ai_campuses.name: Louisiana Cyber Innovation Center (LCIC) — Bossier City
ai_campuses.notes: Public-private cybersecurity R&D hub co-located with defense technology partners near Barksdale AFB; anchors Louisiana's cyber technology corridor.

ai_campuses.name: L3Harris Technologies Mission Operations Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: L3Harris global HQ supports defense electronics, satellite communications, and intelligence systems compute at the Melbourne, FL campus — among the largest defense-tech data footprints in Florida.

ai_campuses.name: US Army NETCOM Data Center — Fort Huachuca
ai_campuses.operator: US Army Network Enterprise Technology Command (NETCOM)
ai_campuses.notes: Primary Army network management and enterprise IT data center; supports Army-wide network operations, defensive cyber, and Army Cyber Command missions.

ai_campuses.name: Purdue University RCAC Computing Cluster — West Lafayette
ai_campuses.notes: One of the largest university HPC environments in the US, hosting Anvil, Bell, and Gilbreth clusters; NSF ACCESS allocation site supporting national research.

ai_campuses.name: Indiana University UITS Data Center (BigRed) — Bloomington
ai_campuses.notes: IU operates BigRed200 (Cray Shasta supercomputer) and Carbonate HPC clusters; one of the most advanced university research networks globally, supporting genomics, HPC, and AI workloads.

ai_campuses.name: CyrusOne Boca Raton Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: CyrusOne carrier-neutral colocation facility serving South Florida enterprise and financial services clients; part of the Boca Raton corporate technology cluster.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon AWS Central Ohio Data Center — Delaware County
ai_campuses.notes: AWS hyperscale data center campus in Delaware County, part of the broader AWS us-east-2 (Ohio) region footprint expanding beyond Franklin County into the lower-cost Delaware County corridor.

ai_campuses.name: WVU High Performance Computing and Research Center (HPCRC)
ai_campuses.notes: WVU HPCRC provides HPC resources for materials science, drug discovery, and energy research; also supports NASA IV&V software verification workflows co-located in Morgantown.

ai_campuses.name: Fort Liberty Army Computing and Mission Command Center
ai_campuses.operator: US Army XVIII Airborne Corps / USASOC
ai_campuses.notes: Primary Army data and mission-command computing hub for the XVIII Airborne Corps and US Army Special Operations Command; supports joint all-domain command and control (JADC2) infrastructure.

ai_campuses.name: W.M. Keck Observatory Data Processing Center
ai_campuses.notes: Keck Observatory processes petabytes of astronomical imaging and spectroscopy data; connected to Caltech and UC system HPC networks for reduction, AI classification, and archival storage.

ai_campuses.name: Mat-Su Borough Digital Infrastructure Hub
ai_campuses.notes: MTA operates fiber network infrastructure and edge computing services for the Mat-Su Valley; supports state broadband connectivity and regional enterprise IT.

ai_campuses.name: UC Davis Farm Computing and Research Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: UC Davis operates research computing supporting the College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences, genomics research, and UC Davis Health clinical data; part of the UC-wide research computing network.

ai_campuses.name: California Department of Technology (CDT) State Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: CDT operates the primary state government data center in Sacramento supporting California's enterprise IT consolidation initiative (CGEN); hosts applications for dozens of state agencies and provides cloud-on-ramp services.

ai_campuses.name: Virginia Information Technologies Agency (VITA) Enterprise Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: VITA's enterprise data center provides shared IT infrastructure and managed services to Virginia state agencies; supports the Commonwealth's digital government transformation and cybersecurity operations.

ai_campuses.name: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI) — Asheville
ai_campuses.operator: NOAA / National Centers for Environmental Information
ai_campuses.notes: NOAA NCEI Asheville is the world's largest archive of atmospheric, coastal, geophysical, and oceanic data; processes and distributes petabytes of climate and weather data annually to researchers, governments, and private sector.

ai_campuses.name: Naval Base Kitsap Cyber and Network Operations Center
ai_campuses.notes: Naval Base Kitsap operates Navy cybersecurity and network operations supporting submarine forces, surface forces, and Puget Sound Naval Shipyard IT; includes NUWC Keyport's undersea warfare data systems.

ai_campuses.name: OU Supercomputing Center for Education and Research (OSCER)
ai_campuses.notes: OSCER operates Schooner (Dell HPC cluster) and supports OU meteorology, geosciences, and engineering research; co-located in Norman with NOAA Storm Prediction Center for weather modeling collaboration.

ai_campuses.name: Eastman Chemical Company Technology and Innovation Center — Kingsport
ai_campuses.notes: Eastman's Kingsport campus operates one of the most extensive industrial AI and process data systems in the US specialty chemicals sector; supports manufacturing optimization, IoT sensor networks, and enterprise ERP across global operations.

ai_campuses.name: Leidos Global Headquarters and Technology Campus — Columbia
ai_campuses.notes: Leidos' Columbia MD headquarters anchors a major defense IT and AI campus; Leidos is among the largest DoD cybersecurity, intelligence, and health IT contractors, operating classified data systems supporting NSA, DHS, and DoD agencies.

ai_campuses.name: Global Payments (TSYS) Payment Technology Data Center — Columbus
ai_campuses.notes: TSYS (Total System Services, now Global Payments) operates its primary payment processing data center in Columbus GA; processes billions of card transactions annually for financial institutions globally.

ai_campuses.name: University of Montana Research Computing — Missoula
ai_campuses.notes: UM operates statewide research computing and the Montana Cyberinfrastructure Network (MontanaCAN) supporting genomics, environmental science, and forestry research; part of the Internet2 research network.

ai_campuses.name: GlobalWafers Semiconductor Silicon Wafer Facility — Albany
ai_campuses.notes: GlobalWafers' Albany OR facility manufactures semiconductor-grade silicon wafers for the global chip supply chain; operations include extensive process automation, quality data systems, and manufacturing AI supporting the Willamette Valley semiconductor ecosystem.

ai_campuses.name: Eglin AFB Air Force Research Laboratory Munitions and Cyber Operations Center
ai_campuses.operator: US Air Force / AFRL / 96th Test Wing
ai_campuses.notes: Eglin AFB's AFRL Munitions Directorate and 96th TW operate extensive weapons test and evaluation data systems; supports the Air Force's developmental and operational test missions across the Gulf Test Range.

ai_campuses.name: Lake Charles LNG and Petrochemical Industrial Operations Data Hub
ai_campuses.operator: Calcasieu Pass LNG / Energy Transfer (Lake Charles LNG)
ai_campuses.notes: Calcasieu Parish hosts co-located LNG liquefaction control systems, refinery process automation, and environmental SCADA for one of the largest petrochemical industrial complexes in North America; handles real-time operations data for multiple major LNG terminals and chemical plants.

ai_campuses.name: Fort Cavazos III Corps Mission Command and Network Operations Center
ai_campuses.operator: US Army III Corps / 1st Cavalry Division
ai_campuses.notes: Fort Cavazos (formerly Fort Hood) III Corps mission command infrastructure supports combined-arms operations, joint all-domain command and control (JADC2) exercises, and Army-wide network operations training for the 1st Cavalry Division and III Corps subordinate units.

ai_campuses.name: Huntington Ingalls Ingalls Shipbuilding Digital Shipyard Operations
ai_campuses.notes: Ingalls Shipbuilding's Pascagoula facility operates digital shipbuilding systems including 3D design (Aveva AVEVA Marine), ship data management, and production control systems; builds US Navy destroyers, amphibious ships, and Coast Guard vessels.

ai_campuses.name: University of South Carolina Research Computing and SC State Data Center — Columbia
ai_campuses.operator: University of South Carolina / SC Division of Technology Operations
ai_campuses.notes: UofSC operates research HPC supporting clinical genomics, public health, and social sciences; co-located data services with SC's state government DTO data center supporting digital government operations.

ai_campuses.name: Hill AFB Ogden Air Logistics Complex (OO-ALC) — Software Engineering
ai_campuses.operator: US Air Force / Ogden Air Logistics Complex
ai_campuses.notes: OO-ALC's Software Engineering Group maintains and modernizes flight control, avionics, and maintenance software for USAF aircraft; F-35 sustainment software ops co-located; one of Air Force's primary sustainment data centers.

ai_campuses.name: Robins AFB Warner Robins Air Logistics Complex (WR-ALC)
ai_campuses.operator: US Air Force / Warner Robins Air Logistics Complex
ai_campuses.notes: WR-ALC maintains C-17, C-5, F-15, and U-2 aircraft and associated avionics, software, and intelligence systems; Air Force Life Cycle Management Center (AFLCMC) manages major acquisition programs from Robins; among largest employers in Georgia.

ai_campuses.name: UVA Rivanna Research Computing and NRAO Headquarters — Charlottesville
ai_campuses.operator: University of Virginia / National Radio Astronomy Observatory
ai_campuses.notes: UVA's Rivanna HPC cluster supports bioinformatics, data science, and clinical AI research at UVA Health; NRAO headquarters coordinates global radio telescope network data including the Very Large Array (VLA) and ALMA data archives.

ai_campuses.name: Fort Riley Mission Command and K-State Biosecurity Research Institute — Manhattan
ai_campuses.operator: US Army 1st Infantry Division / Kansas State University
ai_campuses.notes: Fort Riley hosts 1st Infantry Division mission command and training data systems; K-State's Biosecurity Research Institute (BRI) operates BSL-3 research computing and NBAF (National Bio and Agro-defense Facility) data systems adjacent to campus.

ai_campuses.name: Paducah Area TVA Power Operations and DOE Technology Center
ai_campuses.operator: Tennessee Valley Authority / US DOE Paducah Site
ai_campuses.notes: TVA's Paducah area grid operations manage power distribution for western Kentucky and surrounding states; the DOE Paducah Site's ongoing deactivation and remediation program operates extensive environmental monitoring and process data systems.

ai_campuses.name: NASA Stennis Space Center — Propulsion Test and Data Operations
ai_campuses.notes: Stennis hosts RS-25 SLS engine test stands, National Data Buoy Center oceanographic data operations, and multiple tenant agency computing (NGA, Navy, EPA); largest NASA field installation in the Southeast by land area.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon Web Services West Creek Data Center Campus — Goochland
ai_campuses.notes: AWS's West Creek corridor in Goochland County represents one of the largest hyperscale data center investments in the Richmond metro area; supports us-east-1 region capacity expansion beyond Northern Virginia's constrained market.

ai_campuses.name: Pacific Missile Range Facility (PMRF) — Barking Sands
ai_campuses.notes: PMRF operates the world's largest instrumented, multi-environment missile test range with radar, tracking, and telemetry systems for missile defense, anti-submarine warfare, and undersea warfare testing in the Pacific; hosts MDA and MDA Aegis BMD test data systems.

ai_campuses.name: Fort Sill Army Fires Center of Excellence and JADOC — Lawton
ai_campuses.operator: US Army Fires Center of Excellence
ai_campuses.notes: Fort Sill's Fires Center of Excellence manages joint air defense and field artillery mission command systems, Patriot battery C2, and the Joint Air Defense Operations Center (JADOC) data infrastructure supporting Army IAMD operations.

ai_campuses.name: Vandenberg Space Force Base — Space Launch Delta 30 Mission Control
ai_campuses.operator: US Space Force / Space Launch Delta 30
ai_campuses.notes: Vandenberg SFB's SLD-30 provides launch telemetry, satellite tracking (FPS-16 radar, telemetry stations), and mission control data for DoD, NASA, and commercial polar-orbit launches; SpaceX Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy operations co-located.

ai_campuses.name: Fort Drum 10th Mountain Division Mission Command Center
ai_campuses.notes: Fort Drum's 10th Mountain Division operates mission command and logistics data systems supporting the most-deployed US Army division; includes joint exercise data infrastructure and theater sustainment computing.

ai_campuses.name: Massachusetts Green High Performance Computing Center (MGHPCC) — Holyoke
ai_campuses.notes: MGHPCC is one of the most energy-efficient research data centers in the US, serving research computing for 5 major New England universities; powered by hydroelectric power from the Connecticut River; exemplar of academic research data center design.

ai_campuses.name: Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) Enterprise Data Center — St. Paul
ai_campuses.notes: MNIT's state data center provides enterprise IT infrastructure for all Minnesota state agencies, including cybersecurity operations, cloud services brokerage, and the state's 911 emergency communications system backbone.

ai_campuses.name: Kansas Office of Information Technology Services (OITS) State Data Center — Topeka
ai_campuses.operator: Kansas Office of Information Technology Services
ai_campuses.notes: Kansas OITS operates the state government primary data center supporting agency IT consolidation and shared services; manages the KanAccess broadband mapping and rural connectivity data programs.

ai_campuses.name: Kohler Company Innovation Center and Manufacturing Technology Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Kohler's Sheboygan County campus houses the company's manufacturing AI, IoT sensor integration, and enterprise data systems for global operations; Kohler's residential and hospitality technology platforms process data from millions of connected fixtures worldwide.

ai_campuses.name: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) Roseville Manufacturing and R&D Campus
ai_campuses.notes: HPE's Roseville campus is one of the company's largest US manufacturing facilities, producing server hardware, storage systems, and networking equipment; houses R&D for HPE's Supercomputing and Mission Critical Systems division.

ai_campuses.name: University of Oregon Research Computing and Talapus HPC — Eugene
ai_campuses.notes: UO operates Talapus HPC and research computing supporting genomics, neuroscience, earth sciences, and humanities digital research; connected to Pacific Research Platform (PRP) and Internet2 networks.

ai_campuses.name: Iowa State University CyEnce Research Computing and ISU Research Park — Ames
ai_campuses.operator: Iowa State University / ISU Research Park
ai_campuses.notes: ISU operates CyEnce and Nova HPC clusters supporting agricultural genomics, computational fluid dynamics, and materials science; ISU Research Park co-locates Corteva Agriscience and USDA NADC computational biology with university resources.

ai_campuses.name: NAS Patuxent River Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division (NAWCAD)
ai_campuses.operator: US Navy / Naval Air Systems Command (NAVAIR)
ai_campuses.notes: NAWCAD operates flight test data acquisition systems, aircraft simulation labs, and avionics test data infrastructure for all Navy and Marine Corps aircraft programs; Test Pilot School telemetry and SH-60, F/A-18, and F-35C test data systems co-located.

ai_campuses.name: New Hampshire Bureau of Information Technology Management (BITM) — Concord
ai_campuses.operator: New Hampshire Department of Information Technology
ai_campuses.notes: NH BITM manages the state government data center in Concord providing shared IT services for state agencies, including 911 communications, DMV, and health and human services data systems for New Hampshire's no-income-tax state economy.

ai_campuses.name: NOAA Earth System Research Laboratories (ESRL) — Boulder CO
ai_campuses.notes: NOAA ESRL in Boulder operates high-performance computing systems supporting atmospheric research, climate modeling, and weather-prediction AI. Co-located with NCAR/UCAR Mesa Laboratory on the NOAA-Boulder campus.

ai_campuses.name: Texas Tech University High Performance Computing Center (HPCC)
ai_campuses.notes: TTU HPCC operates Quanah (Cray) and other HPC clusters supporting research in wind energy, climate modeling, atmospheric science, and AI/ML workloads. Part of the National Wind Institute campus.

ai_campuses.name: Naval Air Station Oceana / Commander Navy Region Mid-Atlantic
ai_campuses.notes: NAS Oceana hosts extensive C4ISR and cyber operations infrastructure for the Atlantic Fleet. Commander Navy Region Mid-Atlantic coordinates data/AI operations for naval facilities from Virginia Beach to Maine.

ai_campuses.name: Forsyth County School District Data and AI Operations Center
ai_campuses.notes: One of Georgia's largest and fastest-growing school districts, FCSD operates a substantial on-premise data center and AI-assisted learning analytics platform serving 60,000+ students. The facility anchors local fiber and data center demand in Cumming.

ai_campuses.name: Illinois Department of Innovation and Technology (DoIT) — State Data Center
ai_campuses.notes: The Illinois DoIT State Data Center in Springfield hosts enterprise IT and AI infrastructure for state government agencies, including the Illinois Integrated Eligibility System and statewide cybersecurity operations.

ai_campuses.name: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) — Jacksonville Global HQ
ai_campuses.notes: FIS, one of the world's largest fintech companies, operates its global headquarters and primary data processing centers in Duval County. The facility handles financial transaction processing, real-time payments, and AI-driven fraud detection at scale, anchoring Jacksonville's position as a fintech data center market.

ai_campuses.name: Hammond Indiana University (HIU) / Calumet Research Computing Center
ai_campuses.operator: Purdue University Northwest / Region IV ESC
ai_campuses.notes: Purdue University Northwest (formerly Calumet) hosts regional research computing and data analytics infrastructure serving the Northwest Indiana Calumet Region. The facility supports AI research in manufacturing optimization, steel industry process control, and Great Lakes environmental modeling.

ai_campuses.name: Wolfspeed Marcy Nanotechnology Center — Utica NY
ai_campuses.notes: Wolfspeed's Marcy Nanotechnology Center in Oneida County is the world's largest silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductor wafer fabrication facility. Co-located with SUNY Poly's campus, it drives substantial HPC and AI workloads for process simulation, materials science research, and semiconductor design automation.

ai_campuses.name: Washington State Consolidated Technology Services (CTS) — Olympia
ai_campuses.operator: Washington State Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO)
ai_campuses.notes: WA CTS operates the state's primary data center and shared IT infrastructure from Olympia, serving all Washington state agencies. The facility hosts enterprise AI and analytics platforms including the WA Cares Fund system, DCYF case management AI, and statewide cybersecurity operations (WA-SOC).

ai_campuses.name: Western Michigan University HPC / Kalamazoo Life Sciences Computing
ai_campuses.notes: Western Michigan University operates high-performance computing infrastructure supporting pharmaceutical AI research in partnership with the Kalamazoo life-sciences cluster (Pfizer, Stryker, Zoetis). Research areas include protein folding, clinical trial data analytics, and orthopedic implant design AI.

ai_campuses.name: Idaho National Laboratory (INL) Cybercore Integration Center
ai_campuses.operator: U.S. Department of Energy / Battelle Energy Alliance
ai_campuses.notes: INL's Cybercore Integration Center in Idaho Falls is DOE's premier facility for industrial control system cybersecurity research and AI-driven threat detection. The facility also hosts the Mission Support Center HPC cluster supporting nuclear energy modeling, materials science AI, and critical infrastructure protection.

ai_campuses.name: St. Tammany Corporation Innovation Center — Covington LA
ai_campuses.operator: St. Tammany Corporation / Northshore Tech Hub
ai_campuses.notes: The St. Tammany Corporation operates the Northshore technology innovation hub in Covington, supporting data analytics, cybersecurity, and AI/ML startups serving the Gulf Coast energy and logistics sectors. The facility anchors Northshore Tech, a regional tech cluster attracting remote tech workers from the New Orleans metro.

ai_campuses.name: Fort Liberty (Fort Bragg) USSOCOM Cyber and AI Operations
ai_campuses.notes: Fort Liberty hosts U.S. Army Special Operations Command (USSOCOM) cyber and intelligence computing infrastructure, including classified AI/ML platforms for mission planning, threat analysis, and tactical autonomous systems research. Fort Liberty is one of the largest military installations in the world by population.

ai_campuses.name: Cowlitz County PUD Industrial Computing — Longview WA
ai_campuses.operator: Cowlitz County PUD / Lower Columbia Economic Development Council
ai_campuses.notes: Cowlitz County PUD has partnered with the Lower Columbia EDC to develop an industrial data center park leveraging the PUD's ultra-low hydroelectric rates (<2 cents/kWh). Several hyperscale operators have engaged in site selection processes for the Longview industrial port area, making this a planned major computing campus in the Pacific Northwest.

ai_campuses.name: Arkansas State University High Performance Computing Center — Jonesboro
ai_campuses.notes: Arkansas State University operates a regional HPC cluster and data analytics center in Jonesboro, supporting research in agricultural AI, bioinformatics, and supply-chain optimization for the Arkansas Delta region. The facility is part of the Arkansas High Performance Computing Center (AHPCC) statewide network.

ai_campuses.name: UMass Medical School / WPI Advanced Research Computing Hub — Worcester MA
ai_campuses.operator: University of Massachusetts Medical School / Worcester Polytechnic Institute
ai_campuses.notes: UMass Medical and WPI jointly anchor central Massachusetts's life-sciences computing cluster. Research computing at UMass Medical supports genomics, clinical AI, and NIH-funded biomedical informatics. WPI's Data Science program operates HPC resources shared with regional biotech partners.

ai_campuses.name: Synchrony Financial Data Operations Center — Stamford CT
ai_campuses.notes: Synchrony Financial, one of the nation's largest consumer financial services companies, operates its headquarters and primary data operations center in Stamford. The facility processes credit and financing AI/ML models serving 70M+ active accounts and 450,000+ merchant partners, anchoring Fairfield County's fintech computing cluster.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon Shakopee Fulfillment AI Operations Center
ai_campuses.notes: Amazon operates multiple fulfillment and delivery station facilities in Shakopee (Scott County), among the largest employment sites in the Twin Cities metro. These facilities deploy AI-driven robotics, inventory prediction, and last-mile routing optimization, making them significant edge AI operations nodes.

ai_campuses.name: UC Merced Cyberinfrastructure and HPC Initiative
ai_campuses.notes: UC Merced's Cyberinfrastructure Initiative provides HPC and cloud resources to researchers across UC campuses. Research areas include Central Valley climate modeling, agricultural AI, wildfire prediction, and renewable energy grid optimization. The campus is allocated compute on NSF ACCESS and XSEDE successor systems.

ai_campuses.name: Muscogee (Creek) Nation Tribal Digital Operations Center — Okmulgee OK
ai_campuses.notes: The Muscogee (Creek) Nation operates a centralized tribal government data center in Okmulgee supporting health records (Indian Health Service integration), gaming analytics, citizen services, and the Nation's e-commerce and retail technology platforms for tribal enterprises.

ai_campuses.name: Colorado State University HPC / NCAR Wyoming Supercomputing Liaison — Fort Collins CO
ai_campuses.notes: CSU's high-performance computing cluster in Fort Collins supports atmospheric science, climate modeling, wildfire AI, and agricultural research in partnership with NCAR's Mesa Lab (Boulder) and the NCAR-Wyoming Supercomputing Center (Cheyenne). CSU's data science and AI programs anchor Larimer County's research computing demand.

ai_campuses.name: Library of Congress National Digital Preservation Infrastructure
ai_campuses.notes: The Library of Congress operates one of the largest digital preservation and AI-assisted cataloging infrastructures in the world, with primary systems on Capitol Hill. The LC's National Digital Information Infrastructure and Preservation Program (NDIIPP) and AI Labs for metadata enrichment serve the national digital collections.

ai_campuses.name: Micron Technology Manassas Semiconductor Fab — AI Process Control
ai_campuses.notes: Micron's Manassas fabrication plant (formerly 'IM Flash') is one of the oldest DRAM memory chip fabs in the U.S., now producing LPDDR5 and 3D NAND memory. The facility operates AI-driven process control and defect detection systems, making it one of the most significant semiconductor AI deployments on the East Coast.

ai_campuses.name: Stellantis Brighton Assembly and AI Engineering Center — Livingston County MI
ai_campuses.notes: Stellantis operates engineering and prototype vehicle testing facilities in Livingston County, MI, including AI-driven autonomous vehicle simulation, electrification software testing, and connected-car data processing. The Brighton/Howell area hosts several Tier-1 supplier operations and Stellantis powertrain engineering centers.

ai_campuses.name: Minot Air Force Base — 91st Missile Wing Command and Control
ai_campuses.operator: U.S. Air Force / Air Force Global Strike Command
ai_campuses.notes: Minot AFB hosts the 91st Missile Wing, responsible for Minuteman III ICBMs across 150 launch facilities in ND, and the 5th Bomb Wing (B-52H). The base operates classified nuclear command-and-control computing infrastructure and Air Force Global Strike Command C4ISR systems. Emerging AI/ML applications support targeting analytics and base cybersecurity.

ai_campuses.name: Bell Works Holmdel AI and Technology Campus
ai_campuses.notes: Redevelopment of historic AT&T Bell Labs Holmdel complex (1962 Eero Saarinen building) as a mixed-use technology campus. Hosts AI, cloud, and telecom tenants leveraging the site's fiber infrastructure heritage. New Jersey's largest redeveloped corporate campus.

ai_campuses.name: Portland General Electric Willamette Falls AI Grid Control Center
ai_campuses.notes: Portland General Electric operations and grid control facility in Clackamas County near Oregon City, incorporating AI-driven predictive load management for the Willamette Valley hydro and transmission system. Supports BPA integration and real-time grid optimization.

ai_campuses.name: Minnesota Power AI Grid Operations Center
ai_campuses.notes: Minnesota Power (ALLETE) grid operations center in Grand Rapids serving Itasca County and the Iron Range. Integrates AI-driven load forecasting and renewable dispatch optimization for Minnesota Power's hydro, wind, and biomass generation portfolio serving northern Minnesota.

ai_campuses.name: Amazon Web Services Potomac Yard Federal AI Infrastructure Campus
ai_campuses.notes: AWS federal cloud and AI infrastructure campus in Alexandria's Potomac Yard corridor, adjacent to AWS HQ2 in Arlington. Hosts GovCloud-adjacent AI services for federal agency customers. Part of the Northern Virginia data center cluster with direct federal network peering.

ai_campuses.name: TD SYNNEX AI Distribution and Technology Operations Campus
ai_campuses.notes: TD SYNNEX (formerly Tech Data) major operations campus in Clearwater, Pinellas County FL. AI-driven distribution logistics, IT product configuration, and cloud services integration. One of the largest IT distributor operations centers in North America, serving resellers and enterprise customers.

ai_campuses.name: Texas A&M FASTER Supercomputing and AI Research Cluster
ai_campuses.notes: Texas A&M High Performance Research Computing FASTER system (Dell EMC, AMD EPYC, 2.5 PFLOPs peak) in College Station, Brazos County. Hosts AI/ML workloads for agriculture, energy, materials science, and biomedical research. Part of the NSF ACCESS (formerly XSEDE) national research computing network.

ai_campuses.name: Rogue Valley AI Data Analytics and Research Center
ai_campuses.operator: Southern Oregon University / Rogue Community College
ai_campuses.notes: Regional higher education AI and data analytics research center serving the Rogue Valley (Medford/Ashland/Grants Pass corridor). Jointly operated through Southern Oregon University and Rogue Community College workforce development programs. Supports agricultural AI, forestry monitoring, and Klamath River restoration science computing needs.

ai_campuses.name: Cal Poly San Luis Obispo Center for Computing and AI Research
ai_campuses.notes: Cal Poly SLO's computing and AI research infrastructure supporting the university's Learn by Doing engineering programs. Houses HPC clusters for environmental science, agricultural tech, and structural engineering AI. Operates in proximity to Diablo Canyon nuclear plant's reliable baseload grid zone.

ai_campuses.name: Missouri Western State University AI and Data Science Center
ai_campuses.notes: Missouri Western State University AI and Data Science program hub in St. Joseph (Buchanan County). Partners with regional agriculture, logistics, and healthcare employers on applied AI research. Supported by Missouri's STEM Initiative and Evergy's education partnership programs for grid optimization research.

ai_campuses.name: Rockford University and NIU AI Regional Innovation Hub
ai_campuses.notes: Planned regional AI innovation hub for Winnebago County, a collaboration between Rockford University and Northern Illinois University's Rockford academic programs. Intended to anchor Rockford's I-90 technology corridor with AI-enabled manufacturing analytics for the region's aerospace and automotive supply chain industry.

ai_campuses.name: Chippewa Falls Technology Park — HPE Cray Heritage Computing Site
ai_campuses.operator: Chippewa Falls Technology Park / HPE Cray Alumni Network
ai_campuses.notes: Multi-tenant technology campus occupying the historic Cray Research supercomputer design and manufacturing site in Chippewa Falls. The campus maintains computing heritage and hosts AI, embedded systems, and engineering tenants leveraging the original Cray Research infrastructure legacy. Xcel Energy NSP provides power via legacy high-reliability feeds designed for supercomputer loads.

ai_campuses.name: Northern Kentucky University AI and Data Science Research Center
ai_campuses.notes: NKU's College of Informatics AI and Data Science research center in Highland Heights, Campbell County. Hosts applied AI research for Cincinnati-region healthcare, logistics, and financial services sectors. Partners with Duke Energy Ohio on smart grid AI projects and Greater Cincinnati tech ecosystem programs.

ai_campuses.name: Jacksonville State University Center for Applied AI
ai_campuses.notes: JSU Center for Applied AI in Jacksonville, Calhoun County AL. Focuses on AI applications for defense manufacturing analytics (Fort McClellan area), materials science, and rural healthcare informatics. Partners with Gadsden State Community College and Alabama Power on workforce development programs for technology industry.

ai_campuses.name: University of Wyoming School of Energy Resources AI Research Center
ai_campuses.notes: University of Wyoming's AI research infrastructure supporting the School of Energy Resources wind energy optimization, carbon capture, and clean energy transition research programs. Located in Laramie (Albany County), with HPC systems supporting NCAR/NOAA weather modeling for Wyoming Wind Corridor forecasting and grid integration research.

ai_campuses.name: Basin Electric Power Cooperative AI Energy Management Center
ai_campuses.notes: Basin Electric Power Cooperative grid management and AI energy dispatch center in Beulah, Mercer County ND. Coordinates real-time generation dispatch for Basin Electric's 2.9 GW coal and 1+ GW wind generation portfolio serving 2.9 million people across nine states. AI-driven load forecasting supports coal-to-renewable transition planning.

ai_campuses.name: Fort Knox U.S. Army Cyber and AI Operations Center
ai_campuses.operator: U.S. Army Network Enterprise Technology Command
ai_campuses.notes: Fort Knox (Hardin County KY) hosts U.S. Army NETCOM regional operations and Cyber Institute training facilities. AI-enabled threat detection, network monitoring, and autonomous systems testing programs. Supports the Army's Project Convergence AI integration initiative for combined arms operations.

ai_campuses.name: Mississippi State University High Performance Computing Collaboratory
ai_campuses.notes: MSU's High Performance Computing Collaboratory (HPCC) in Starkville, Oktibbeha County MS. Operates SuperMike-II and specialized GPU clusters for AI/ML research in agriculture, materials science, and biomedical engineering. Hosts the NSF-funded Center for Air Sea Technology and agricultural AI programs for the Mississippi Delta.

ai_campuses.name: Savannah River National Laboratory AI-Enabled Nuclear Science Center
ai_campuses.operator: Savannah River National Laboratory / DOE NNSA
ai_campuses.notes: SRNL AI research center in Aiken County SC, operating under DOE's Office of Environmental Management. Applies AI and machine learning to nuclear materials monitoring, environmental remediation prediction, and radioactive waste characterization at the Savannah River Site. Part of DOE's National Laboratory AI Initiative.

ai_campuses.name: Rowan-Cabarrus Community College Advanced Technology and AI Center
ai_campuses.notes: Rowan-Cabarrus Community College AI and advanced manufacturing technology center in Salisbury, Rowan County NC. Trains data center operations technicians, network engineers, and AI-assisted manufacturing workers for the Charlotte-Greensboro industrial corridor. Partners with Duke Energy Carolinas and Fibrant municipal broadband for hands-on grid and network training.

ai_campuses.name: Dominion Energy North Anna Nuclear AI Power Management System
ai_campuses.notes: Dominion Energy's AI-enabled grid management and nuclear plant operations support system at North Anna Power Station, Louisa County VA. Integrates predictive maintenance AI for nuclear components, real-time grid dispatch optimization, and load forecasting for the PJM Virginia zone. Part of Dominion's broader grid modernization under Virginia's Clean Economy Act.

ai_campuses.name: Penn State Institute for Computational and Data Sciences — Roar HPC
ai_campuses.notes: Penn State ICDS Roar and Roar Collab high-performance computing systems in State College, Centre County PA. 28,000+ CPU cores, 100+ GPU nodes including NVIDIA A100s for AI/ML research. Supports Penn State faculty across 24 colleges in areas including materials science, drug discovery, astrophysics, and agricultural AI. Part of NSF ACCESS allocation network.

ai_campuses.name: Wilkes University AI and Engineering Research Center
ai_campuses.notes: Wilkes University engineering and computing research center in Wilkes-Barre, Luzerne County PA. AI research focuses on smart grid optimization for the northeastern Pennsylvania grid, predictive manufacturing for the regional industrial sector, and healthcare informatics for Geisinger Health System partnerships. Leverages PPL Electric Utilities grid data partnerships.

ai_campuses.name: Youngstown State University Advanced Manufacturing AI Research Center
ai_campuses.notes: YSU's Williamson College of Business Administration and STEM College joint AI research center in Youngstown, Mahoning County OH. Focuses on AI applications for steel and metals manufacturing automation, additive manufacturing process optimization, and regional economic transformation analytics. Partners with Youngstown's Additive Manufacturing incubator cluster.

ai_campuses.name: Franklin College Center for Data Analytics and AI
ai_campuses.notes: Franklin College AI and data analytics research center in Franklin, Johnson County IN. Focuses on applied AI for healthcare informatics (Johnson Memorial Health partnership), agricultural technology for southern Indiana farming operations, and supply chain optimization for Indianapolis-area logistics industry. Supported by Lilly Endowment higher education grants.

ai_campuses.name: Hancock Regional Hospital AI Clinical Decision Support System
ai_campuses.notes: Hancock Regional Hospital's AI-powered clinical decision support and healthcare analytics platform in Greenfield, Hancock County IN. AI systems support emergency department triage prioritization, sepsis prediction, readmission risk scoring, and operational scheduling optimization. Part of the Indiana Regional Medical Center AI Health Initiative network.

ai_campuses.name: Thor Industries / Forest River RV Manufacturing AI Center — Elkhart County IN
ai_campuses.notes: Elkhart County's RV manufacturing cluster — anchored by Thor Industries and Forest River — operates AI-driven design, supply chain, and telematics analytics systems supporting approximately 80% of U.S. recreational vehicle production. Computing operations span Thor's Airstream, Keystone, and Grand Design brands and Forest River's 50+ subsidiary brands.

ai_campuses.name: Cummins Inc. Seymour Engine Manufacturing AI Hub — Jackson County IN
ai_campuses.notes: Cummins Inc.'s Seymour, Indiana engine manufacturing campus operates AI-based production quality control, predictive maintenance, and engine performance simulation systems for diesel and natural gas engines shipped globally. Seymour is one of Cummins' largest manufacturing facilities and a center of the company's digital manufacturing transformation.

ai_campuses.name: Los Alamos National Laboratory Trinity/Crossroads Supercomputer — Los Alamos County NM
ai_campuses.notes: LANL's Trinity system (formerly ranked #6 globally at 41.5 PFLOPS) and its successor Crossroads support nuclear weapons stockpile stewardship simulation, materials science, climate modeling, and national security AI/ML workloads under the National Nuclear Security Administration. LANL is co-managed by Triad National Security LLC (Battelle/University of California/Texas A&M).

ai_campuses.name: Johnston County Research Triangle Industrial AI Park — Johnston County NC
ai_campuses.notes: Johnston County is developing an AI and advanced manufacturing industrial park along the US-70 corridor to capture Research Triangle Park overflow demand. Anchor tenants include distribution and light manufacturing operations supported by Duke Energy Progress industrial power. Proximity to Raleigh-Durham fiber rings makes the county attractive for edge computing and disaster recovery.

ai_campuses.name: Toyota Motor Manufacturing Mississippi (TMMMS) AI Production System — Union County MS
ai_campuses.notes: Toyota's Blue Springs, Mississippi plant (TMMMS) produces the Corolla sedan and employs approximately 2,000 workers. The facility operates Toyota's Toyota Production System (TPS) AI quality inspection, supply chain analytics, and connected vehicle data processing systems, representing one of the most significant automotive AI deployments in the Deep South.

ai_campuses.name: General Motors Spring Hill Cadillac Lyriq EV AI Manufacturing — Maury County TN
ai_campuses.notes: GM's Spring Hill Manufacturing complex was retooled with a $2 billion investment to produce the Cadillac Lyriq EV and LYRIQ-V, becoming one of the most advanced AI-enabled automotive manufacturing facilities in the U.S. The plant operates computer vision quality systems, battery management AI, and connected vehicle over-the-air update infrastructure.

ai_campuses.name: Shelby County Alabama Technology Corridor — Birmingham Metro South
ai_campuses.notes: Shelby County's US-31 and I-65 technology corridor hosts Amazon fulfillment AI robotics operations, Grandview Medical Center's clinical AI systems, and a dense cluster of professional services firms serving the Birmingham metro. The corridor benefits from Alabama Power's competitive industrial rates and the county's status as Alabama's fastest-growing community.
